baseline_dominance.justification: The AS-adversary framing (§1, four production facts) gives Bolyra real separation: properties that hold under AS compromise are outside vanilla OAuth's envelope. However, auth0_pm Attack 4 shows Client Attestation draft closes H2 without ZK; rfc7662_advocate Attack 2 shows signed JWT introspection closes H1's human-binding; rfc7662_advocate Attack 4 demonstrates H5's baseline characterization of RFC 8693 is factually incorrect (may_act pre-authorization enables local validation without per-hop AS roundtrip); spiffe_engineer Attack 3 shows SPIFFE federation already provides H3's portability. The 'general case' strict dominance claim does not survive — the strongest differentiators (GGM puncturing, AS-adversary resistance) are real but niche, not general-case. Baseline can approximate most stated properties with extra configuration.

formal_security_argument.justification: Threat model is explicit (AS-adversary with Okta 2023, Auth0 S-1, RFC 9701 gap, 18 U.S.C. §2703(d)). Named assumptions: SE-PLONK under ROM, Poseidon2 PRF, DDH for Pedersen. Six formal games with reduction sketches. However, cryptographer Attack 2 exposes a fundamental gap: COMPOSE-EXTRACT (Game 6) assumes nonce binding between human and agent legs but does not specify whether the nonce is committed in-circuit or merely declared as public input — if public-input-only, cross-circuit malleability breaks the atomic mutual proof (H1). Cryptographer Attack 1 shows FS-NULL (Game 2) does not model key logging before puncturing under the legal compulsion adversary that §1 itself motivates. Cryptographer Attack 3 shows the bearer fallback in §7 has no formal anti-downgrade treatment. Reductions exist but have material soundness gaps.

implementability.justification: Both human and agent legs map to PLONK (existing Bolyra primitive). GGM tree uses Poseidon2, Pedersen re-randomization at ~2,100 constraints. §2.8 gives concrete latency budget: cold auth ~4s (native parallel), per-tool-call <1ms (session token), 3-hop delegation ~6s (parallel PLONK). Hardware target M2+ ARM64. Session TTL = 1hr amortizes cold start. §7 specifies ~380-line drop-in middleware adapter. Halo2-style recursion for chains ≥3. All primitives are within Phase 1 Bolyra stack with stated circuit costs.

adversarial_survival.justification: Tier 3 with all 5 personas surfaced multiple attacks the construction cannot answer. Critical unanswered attacks: (1) cryptographer Attack 2 — COMPOSE-EXTRACT nonce binding underspecification allows cross-circuit malleability, breaking H1's atomic mutual proof; (2) cryptographer Attack 3 — bearer fallback is a universal downgrade path with no anti-downgrade mechanism; (3) auth0_pm Attack 1 — session token is an unbound bearer token for 99.9% of the TTL window, collapsing all ZK guarantees mid-session; (4) cu_ciso Attack 1 — unlinkability by construction is a GLBA Safeguards Rule failure (no attributable audit trail); (5) cu_ciso Attack 2 — human_sk custody entirely unspecified (no recovery, no device migration); (6) rfc7662_advocate Attack 1 — PLONK verifying key distribution is an unaddressed trust root with no revocation or upgrade lifecycle; (7) rfc7662_advocate Attack 4 — H5's baseline characterization of RFC 8693 is factually incorrect. Many attacks marked 'No — not in threat model' by the attackers themselves, meaning the construction has no answer.

scenario_fit.justification: Two scenarios named: 'generic Claude agent talking to third-party MCP server' and 'cross-vendor agent handoff (Claude → ChatGPT).' These are plausible but hypothetical — no named credit union, no named enterprise stakeholder, no specific regulatory requirement making the property load-bearing. The cu_ciso attacks (Attacks 1, 4) highlight that no FFIEC CAT mapping, no NCUA Part 748 compliance narrative, and no concrete CU deployment exists. The scenarios describe desirable agent-economy topology but lack the specificity of a named deployment where ZK properties are regulatory-mandated rather than nice-to-have.

gaps: Session token must be cryptographically bound to the PLONK proof transcript (e.g., DPoP-style proof-of-possession keyed to the ZK witness) — otherwise ZK guarantees evaporate for 99.9% of the session TTL
COMPOSE-EXTRACT (Game 6) must specify in-circuit nonce commitment: each circuit's witness must include a hash of the counterpart's public input vector to prevent cross-circuit malleability
Add anti-downgrade mechanism for bearer fallback: either a mandatory Bolyra-Only mode or DPoP-style binding that makes the Bearer token non-transferable without the ZK witness

Correct H5's baseline characterization: RFC 8693 §4.4 may_act pre-authorization enables local chain validation without per-hop AS roundtrip — find the specific delegation topology RFC 8693 cannot handle
Address PLONK verifying-key distribution as a first-class trust operation with revocation, versioning, and authenticated distribution channel — or retract H3 zero-config claim
Specify human_sk key custody: device storage model, recovery path, HSM option, and device migration — operationally undeployable without this
Add selective-disclosure or audit-mode path reconciling unlinkability with GLBA Safeguards Rule §314.4(c)(3) attributable audit requirements
Add explicit comparison in §2.3 showing why SPIRE SVID rotation is insufficient under AS-adversary (CA compromise during rotation window)
Name a concrete deployment with a specific credit union or enterprise stakeholder where the ZK properties are regulatory-mandated, not nice-to-have
Produce FFIEC CAT / NIST CSF control mapping table for examiner defensibility
